m	Z	m
)	zUfontTools.pens.pointInsidePen -- Pen implementing "point inside" testing
for shapes.
print_function
division
absolute_import)
BasePen)
solveQuadratic
solveCubic
PointInsidePenc
This pen implements "point inside" testing: to test whether
	a given point lies inside the shape (black) or outside (white).
	Instances of this class can be recycled, as long as the
	setTestPoint() method is used to set the new point to test.
	Typical usage:
		pen = PointInsidePen(glyphSet, (100, 200))
		outline.draw(pen)
		isInside = pen.getResult()
	Both the even-odd algorithm and the non-zero-winding-rule
	algorithm are implemented. The latter is the default, specify
	True for the evenOdd argument of __init__ or setTestPoint
	to use the even-odd algorithm.
__init__
setTestPoint)
selfZ
glyphSet
	testPoint
evenOdd

zASet the point to test. Call this _before_ the outline gets drawn.Nr
firstPoint
intersectionCount)
PointInsidePen.setTestPointc
	closePathr
getWinding6
PointInsidePen.getWindingc
After the shape has been drawn, getResult() returns True if the test
		point lies within the (black) shape, and False if it doesn't.
winding
resultr
	getResult=
